title: Kotta Paakkum Song Lyrics Nattamai
stanzas:
thamarai poovum irukku
sandhana poovum irukku
rendula onnu paakkattumaa

kathiri veyilil kodhippadhu pole
kaaichal adikkuthu
iduppuku melae

Vethala potta udhattil
muththira ida thuninji
chithiravatha seyya poriyaa

vethala kaiyil eduthu
munnum pinnum thodachi
kaambu killi thaaren vaariyaa

suththi varuvadhu saathikkathaane
sundhari azhagu saamikkuthaane
karpooram edhuthu kaamikkathaane
kattilum edhukku saathikkathaane

machaan naanum
sundhari neeyum
thottaa dhool parakkum
thottaa dhool parakkum
thottaa dhool parakkum

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kotta Paakkum?

The song is an energetic and celebratory number that captures the essence of joy and festivity.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song is sung by Mano and S. Janaki.

Who composed the music for Nattamai?

The music for the movie was composed by Sirpy.

What role does this song play in the movie?

The song adds a lively and festive mood to the film, enhancing scenes of celebration and community gatherings.

When was Nattamai released?

The movie was released in the year 1994.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Kotta Paakkum from the movie Nattamai is a lively and energetic track that brings out the spirit of celebration. Sung by Mano and S. Janaki, the song is set against a backdrop of festivity, capturing the essence of joy and community. The lyrics by Vairamuthu, combined with Sirpy's vibrant music, create an atmosphere of happiness and excitement. This duet is perfect for parties and festive occasions, evoking a sense of togetherness and merriment.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the vibrant energy of Kotta Paakkum from the iconic movie Nattamai. This lively duet, featuring the voices of Mano and S. Janaki, is a perfect blend of Sirpy's dynamic music and Vairamuthu's vivid lyrics. Dive into a world of celebration and joy with this unforgettable track!
stanzaMeanings: If you chew betel leaves with areca nut, your mouth will turn red.
Friend, when you and I touch, sparks will fly.
To make the tongue red, you need lime; to make me red, I need a bride.
After tying the wedding knot, I want to dance.
There is a lotus flower and a sandalwood flower; can we see one of them?
All your imaginations are certain; will everything you see come true?
Hidden desires make the eyebrows twitch.
Like burning in the hot sun, fever rises above the waist.
If you put betel leaves on the lips, will you draw a picture with the juice?
Taking betel leaves in hand, rubbing them back and forth, I'll break a twig and give it to you.
Going around doesn't achieve anything; beauty is like a deity; showing camphor doesn't achieve anything; why achieve anything in bed?
When the verse ends, it's the chorus again.
If you chew betel leaves with areca nut, your mouth will turn red.
Friend, when you and the beautiful one touch, sparks will fly.
To make the tongue red, you need lime; to make me red, I need a bride.
After tying the wedding knot, I want to dance.
If you chew betel leaves with areca nut, your mouth will turn red.
Friend, when you and the beautiful one touch, sparks will fly, sparks will fly, sparks will fly.
